mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is above 9%, it indicates significant labor market slack and weak consumer demand, which depresses corporate earnings and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 9%, or rank IC of UNRATE on forward returns is positive over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output and economic growth, which boosts corporate profits and drives positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 60-day forward returns show negative correlation with INDPRO changes over any 3-year window, or Rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales increase month-over-month and the federal funds rate is near zero, the combination of strong consumer demand and loose monetary policy significantly boosts earnings expectations and equity valuations, leading to positive 20-day forward returns.
regime_hint: Stimulative monetary policy with consumer strength
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, SPX 20-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) or negative returns occur more than 40% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: During a period of extremely high unemployment (above 9%) that is starting to decline, markets anticipate labor market healing and eventual Fed tightening, but in the near term the improving job picture supports consumer confidence and spending, driving positive equity returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 9% and UNRATE is lower than its value 3 months ago
failure_condition: When UNRATE >9% and declining, SPX 20-day returns are negative more than 50% of the time or rank IC is negative.

mechanism_narrative: A strong monthly increase in industrial production (above 0.5%) signals robust manufacturing activity, which often leads to positive short-term equity returns as investors react to the growth surprise.
failure_condition: When INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5%, SPX 5-day forward returns are not positive on average, or the hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts indicate increased residential investment and consumer confidence, which supports broader economic growth and justifies higher equity prices over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 60-day forward returns show negative correlation with HOUST changes over any 3-year window, or Rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When both retail sales and industrial production are rising simultaneously, it confirms a broad-based economic expansion across consumption and manufacturing, which strongly supports corporate earnings and leads to positive equity returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0% and INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0%
failure_condition: When both positive growth conditions hold, SPX 20-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or negative returns occur more than 40% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation as measured by CPI rises above 2% year-over-year, it erodes real purchasing power and forces the Fed to eventually raise rates, which compresses equity valuations and leads to negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: When CPI YoY exceeds 2%, SPX 5-day forward returns are positive on average more often than negative, or rank IC is positive.
